Title: Complete outpatient flow register to checkout with single visit context
Preconditions: Registration clerk, clinician, lab tech, pharmacist and cashier accounts exist; outpatient location configured
Test_Steps: 1. Register new patient as clerk | 2. Check in patient and start outpatient visit | 3. Clinician records vitals encounter | 4. Clinician records diagnosis | 5. Clinician orders a lab test | 6. Lab tech enters result | 7. Clinician reviews result and prescribes drug | 8. Pharmacist dispenses | 9. Cashier bills and patient checks out (visit ended)
Test_Data: Patient=Synthetic Jane Doe DOB 1990-01-01; Diagnosis=Malaria; Lab=Malaria smear; Drug=Artemether-Lumefantrine
Expected_Result: Single visit threads all encounters; each encounter carries same visit UUID, correct provider, datetime; visit ends at checkout; dashboard reflects full chronology

Title: Check-in blocked when patient has unresolved active visit at another location
Preconditions: Patient already has an open active visit at Location A
Test_Steps: 1. Search the patient | 2. Attempt to start a new visit at Location B | 3. Observe system response
Test_Data: Patient with open visit at Outpatient-A; New location=Outpatient-B
Expected_Result: System prevents or warns about a second concurrent active visit; clerk is guided to close or transfer the existing visit; no orphan duplicate visit created

Title: Vitals-to-diagnosis handoff preserves visit and provider context
Preconditions: Active outpatient visit with a recorded vitals encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Confirm vitals encounter present | 3. Start a visit-note/diagnosis encounter | 4. Save diagnosis | 5. Inspect both encounters
Expected_Result: Both encounters share the same visit; diagnosis encounter records ordering provider; chronology ordered by encounter datetime

Title: Order lab then result then clinician review state transitions
Preconditions: Active visit; lab order types configured; lab tech account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician places lab order (state=ORDERED) | 2. Lab receives specimen (state=IN_PROGRESS) | 3. Lab enters result (state=COMPLETED) | 4. Clinician reviews and acknowledges | 5. Attempt illegal transition COMPLETED back to ORDERED
Expected_Result: Order traverses ORDERED to IN_PROGRESS to COMPLETED; review records reviewer and timestamp; illegal backward transition is rejected

Title: Prescribe drug with active allergy triggers interaction alert before dispense
Preconditions: Patient has documented penicillin allergy; clinician on active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard, confirm allergy listed | 2. Order amoxicillin | 3. Observe allergy alert | 4. Attempt to override without reason | 5. Provide override reason and proceed
Expected_Result: Allergy alert is raised before order is finalized; override requires documented reason captured in audit; order not silently saved

Title: Dispense quantity must not exceed prescribed quantity boundary
Preconditions: Active prescription for 30 tablets exists; pharmacist account
Test_Steps: 1. Open dispensing queue | 2. Select prescription | 3. Dispense exactly 30 | 4. New prescription 30, attempt dispense 31 | 5. Attempt dispense 0
Expected_Result: Dispense of 30 succeeds; 31 rejected (exceeds prescribed); 0 rejected (non-positive); remaining balance accurate

Title: Billing aggregates all chargeable items from the visit
Preconditions: Visit with consultation, lab order, and dispensed drug; cashier role; billing module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. End clinical activity | 2. Open billing for the visit | 3. Verify line items | 4. Apply payment | 5. Generate receipt
Expected_Result: Bill contains all chargeable items with correct unit prices and totals; payment recorded; receipt generated with visit reference

Title: Checkout closes visit and finalizes encounter datetimes
Preconditions: Active visit with multiple encounters; bill settled
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm visit active | 2. Trigger checkout/end visit | 3. Verify visit stop datetime set | 4. Attempt to add encounter to closed visit
Expected_Result: Visit transitions ACTIVE to ENDED with stop datetime; adding encounter to ended visit is blocked or requires reopening with audit

Title: Admit patient from outpatient visit and assign ward bed
Preconditions: Inpatient program/bed management configured; clinician with admit privilege
Test_Steps: 1. From active outpatient visit, initiate admission | 2. Select ward and bed | 3. Confirm admission encounter | 4. Verify visit type changes to inpatient | 5. Verify bed marked occupied
Expected_Result: Admission encounter recorded; visit reflects inpatient context; bed status occupied; patient appears in ward census

Title: Bed double-assignment prevented (concurrency)
Preconditions: Bed A-12 available; two clinicians attempting assignment simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician 1 selects bed A-12 | 2. Clinician 2 selects bed A-12 | 3. Both submit near-simultaneously
Expected_Result: Only one assignment succeeds; the second receives a conflict/already-occupied error; no two patients share one bed

Title: Inpatient medication order with scheduled administration times
Preconditions: Admitted patient on ward; drug order types configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open admitted patient | 2. Order scheduled drug (e.g., q8h) | 3. Verify administration schedule generated | 4. Nurse records one administration | 5. Verify MAR reflects given dose
Expected_Result: Order creates schedule; nurse administration recorded against scheduled time; MAR shows given/pending statuses accurately

Title: Ward transfer preserves orders and active visit
Preconditions: Admitted patient with active orders in Ward A
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient | 2. Initiate transfer to Ward B | 3. Select new bed | 4. Verify old bed freed and new bed occupied | 5. Verify active orders persist
Test_Data: From=Ward A bed A-12; To=Ward B bed B-03
Expected_Result: Transfer encounter recorded; old bed released, new bed occupied; active orders remain active under same visit

Title: Discharge requires resolution of active orders and bed release
Preconditions: Admitted patient with one active drug order and an open lab order
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate discharge with active orders present | 2. Observe discharge gating | 3. Discontinue/complete active orders | 4. Complete discharge | 5. Verify bed released
Test_Data: Active drug order + open lab order
Expected_Result: Discharge gated while critical orders open (or warns per config); after resolution discharge completes; bed released; discharge encounter recorded

Title: Discharge summary generated with diagnoses and discharge meds
Preconditions: Patient ready for discharge with diagnoses and continuing meds
Test_Steps: 1. Open discharge workflow | 2. Generate discharge summary | 3. Verify diagnoses included | 4. Verify discharge medications listed | 5. Finalize and lock summary
Expected_Result: Discharge summary contains diagnoses, discharge meds, follow-up; finalized summary is immutable/audited

Title: Length-of-stay boundary at same-day admit and discharge
Preconditions: Patient admitted and discharged same calendar day
Test_Steps: 1. Admit at 08:00 | 2. Discharge at 14:00 same day | 3. Inspect LOS computation
Expected_Result: LOS computed correctly (e.g., 0 days or 1 day per business rule), no negative or null LOS

Title: ER triage assigns acuity level and routes to treatment
Preconditions: Emergency location configured; triage nurse and ER clinician accounts
Test_Steps: 1. Register/quick-register arriving patient | 2. Start emergency visit | 3. Record triage vitals and acuity | 4. Route to ER clinician queue | 5. Clinician opens and treats
Expected_Result: Triage encounter records acuity; patient appears in ER queue ordered by acuity; clinician can open and treat under same visit

Title: Triage queue ordering by acuity then arrival time
Preconditions: Multiple ER patients with varying acuity and arrival times
Test_Steps: 1. Triage patient X ESI-3 at 09:00 | 2. Triage patient Y ESI-1 at 09:05 | 3. Triage patient Z ESI-3 at 08:55 | 4. View ER queue order
Expected_Result: Queue order: Y (ESI1) first, then Z (ESI3 earlier), then X; acuity dominates, arrival breaks ties

Title: Unidentified emergency patient registered with John/Jane Doe placeholder
Preconditions: Unconscious unidentified patient arriving; emergency registration enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Quick-register without full demographics | 2. System assigns temporary identifier | 3. Start emergency visit | 4. Later merge/update with real identity
Expected_Result: Temporary identity created with unique placeholder ID; care proceeds; later identity reconciliation merges records without data loss

Title: ER to inpatient admission conversion within same visit
Preconditions: ER patient treated, decision to admit made
Test_Steps: 1. Treat ER patient | 2. Initiate admit from ER | 3. Assign ward bed | 4. Verify visit continuity
Expected_Result: Visit continues uninterrupted; admission encounter added; ER and inpatient encounters share single visit; no duplicate visit

Title: Left-without-being-seen (LWBS) disposition closes visit
Preconditions: ER patient triaged but leaves before clinician seen
Test_Steps: 1. Triage patient | 2. Mark disposition LWBS | 3. Close emergency visit | 4. Verify reporting flag
Expected_Result: Visit closed with LWBS disposition; no treatment encounters; patient counted in LWBS metric for reporting

Title: Outbound referral generated with reason and target facility
Preconditions: Clinician on active visit; referral target facilities configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Create outbound referral | 3. Select target facility and specialty | 4. Add reason and attachments | 5. Submit referral
Test_Data: Target=District Hospital Cardiology; Reason=Suspected MI for cath
Expected_Result: Referral created with reason, target, attachments; status SUBMITTED; referral appears in tracking list

Title: Referral lifecycle state transitions submitted to accepted to completed
Preconditions: Outbound referral in SUBMITTED state
Test_Steps: 1. Receiving facility marks ACCEPTED | 2. Patient seen, mark IN_PROGRESS | 3. Outcome returned, mark COMPLETED | 4. Attempt to re-open COMPLETED referral
Test_Data: Referral states: SUBMITTED to ACCEPTED to IN_PROGRESS to COMPLETED
Expected_Result: Each valid transition recorded with actor and timestamp; reopening completed referral blocked or audited

Title: Referral rejection returns patient to originating provider queue
Preconditions: Referral submitted to facility lacking capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Receiving facility marks REJECTED with reason | 2. Originating provider notified | 3. Provider re-routes to alternate facility
Expected_Result: Rejection captured with reason; originator notified; patient re-routable; original referral retains audit trail

Title: FHIR ServiceRequest represents outbound referral correctly
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; referral created
Test_Steps: 1. Create referral | 2. GET ServiceRequest resource | 3. Validate intent, status, subject, requester, performerType | 4. Validate reasonCode coding
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest validates against R4; subject references Patient; requester references Practitioner; reasonCode uses standard coding system

Title: Visit-encounter referential integrity after encounter void
Preconditions: Visit with three encounters; data manager access
Test_Steps: 1. Void one encounter with reason | 2. Verify encounter marked voided not deleted | 3. Verify visit still references remaining encounters | 4. Verify obs of voided encounter also voided
Expected_Result: Voided encounter retains row with voided flag, reason, voidedBy; child obs voided; visit integrity preserved; no hard delete

Title: Lab result auto-flags critical value and notifies ordering provider
Preconditions: Lab order placed; critical value ranges configured; notifications enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Lab enters a critical-range result | 2. System flags critical | 3. Ordering provider notified | 4. Provider acknowledges
Expected_Result: Result flagged critical; ordering provider receives notification; acknowledgement recorded with timestamp; unacknowledged criticals escalate

Title: Result entry rejected for cancelled/discontinued order
Preconditions: Lab order placed then discontinued by clinician
Test_Steps: 1. Place lab order | 2. Clinician discontinues order | 3. Lab tech attempts to enter result | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Result entry blocked or warns that order is discontinued; no result attaches to a cancelled order silently

Title: Pairwise visit type, location, provider role, order type combinations
Preconditions: Multiple visit types, locations, provider roles, and order types configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise matrix of (visit type x location x provider role x order type) | 2. For each combo place an order | 3. Verify acceptance/denial per RBAC and config
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination behaves per configuration; unauthorized role/order combos denied; authorized combos succeed; no crashes

Title: Duplicate diagnosis on same visit deduped or warned
Preconditions: Active visit with one diagnosis recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record diagnosis Hypertension | 2. Record same diagnosis again on same visit | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: System warns of duplicate or dedupes; does not silently store identical conflicting confirmed diagnoses without indication

Title: Backdated encounter cannot precede visit start datetime
Preconditions: Active visit started at 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. Add encounter dated 09:00 (before visit start) | 2. Add encounter dated 10:30 (within) | 3. Add encounter dated in future
Expected_Result: Encounter before visit start rejected or adjusts visit start per rule; future-dated encounter blocked; within-visit accepted

Title: Concurrent edits to same encounter by two clinicians
Preconditions: Encounter open for edit by two users
Test_Steps: 1. User A opens encounter for edit | 2. User B opens same encounter | 3. User A saves | 4. User B saves stale copy
Expected_Result: Optimistic locking or last-write detection prevents silent overwrite; User B warned of stale data; no lost update

Title: FHIR Encounter reflects visit lifecycle status mapping
Preconditions: FHIR R4 enabled; active then ended visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start visit; GET Encounter status | 2. End visit; GET Encounter status again | 3. Validate status mapping
Expected_Result: Active visit maps to Encounter.status=in-progress; ended maps to finished; class and period populated correctly

Title: HL7 v2 ADT-A04 register, A01 admit, A03 discharge sequence
Preconditions: HL7 inbound/outbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Register patient, capture ADT^A04 | 2. Admit, capture ADT^A01 | 3. Discharge, capture ADT^A03 | 4. Validate PID, PV1 segments and message sequence
Test_Data: ADT messages for one patient journey
Expected_Result: Each event emits correct ADT trigger; PID identifiers consistent; PV1 patient class transitions O to I; message control IDs unique

Title: HL7 v2 ORM/OML lab order and ORU result round-trip
Preconditions: Lab interface configured; LIS simulator available
Test_Steps: 1. Place lab order; capture outbound OML^O21 | 2. LIS returns ORU^R01 result | 3. System ingests ORU | 4. Verify result attached to original order
Expected_Result: Outbound order maps OBR correctly; inbound ORU matched via placer order number; result attaches to correct order/patient

Title: FHIR MedicationRequest to MedicationDispense linkage
Preconditions: FHIR R4 enabled; prescription and dispense exist
Test_Steps: 1. Create prescription; GET MedicationRequest | 2. Dispense; GET MedicationDispense | 3. Validate authorizingPrescription reference
Expected_Result: MedicationDispense.authorizingPrescription references the MedicationRequest; quantities consistent; subject matches

Title: FHIR DiagnosticReport links Observation results to order
Preconditions: Lab order completed with results; FHIR enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET DiagnosticReport for completed lab | 2. Validate result references | 3. Validate based-on ServiceRequest | 4. Validate LOINC coding
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport.result references Observations; basedOn references ServiceRequest; components use LOINC codes

Title: Cross-patient data leakage prevented across visit context switch
Preconditions: Clinician viewing Patient A; Patient B exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Patient A dashboard | 2. Capture order/encounter URLs/IDs | 3. Manipulate ID to reference Patient B's encounter | 4. Observe
Test_Data: Patient A and B encounter IDs
Expected_Result: Authorization enforced server-side; cross-patient object reference denied (no IDOR); attempt logged

Title: Break-the-glass emergency access audited and time-boxed
Preconditions: Restricted patient record; emergency override feature enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician lacking normal access requests break-the-glass | 2. Provide justification | 3. Access granted temporarily | 4. Verify audit and expiry
Expected_Result: Emergency access granted with mandatory justification; access time-boxed; full audit entry created; access auto-revokes

Title: End-to-end audit trail reconstructs full journey
Preconditions: Completed outpatient journey from TC-E2E2-0001
Test_Steps: 1. Open audit log filtered by patient | 2. Verify entries for register, visit, vitals, diagnosis, order, result, prescribe, dispense, bill | 3. Verify actor, timestamp, action for each
Expected_Result: Audit log contains chronological entries for every state-changing action with actor, timestamp, and action type; no gaps

Title: Vitals boundary values across pediatric and adult ranges
Preconditions: Active visit; vitals form with range validation
Test_Steps: 1. Enter age-appropriate normal vitals | 2. Enter physiologically impossible vitals | 3. Enter boundary min/max
Expected_Result: Impossible values rejected/flagged; boundary plausibility validated against age; valid extremes accepted with optional confirmation

Title: Weight-based pediatric dose calculation guardrail
Preconditions: Pediatric patient with recorded weight; weight-based drug
Test_Steps: 1. Record weight | 2. Order weight-based drug | 3. Verify computed dose | 4. Enter dose exceeding max mg/kg | 5. Omit weight and retry
Expected_Result: Dose computed from weight; overdose beyond max mg/kg blocked/warned; missing weight blocks weight-based dosing

Title: Drug-drug interaction across two active prescriptions
Preconditions: Patient on warfarin; clinician prescribing interacting drug
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm active warfarin | 2. Order aspirin | 3. Observe interaction alert | 4. Document override reason
Expected_Result: Drug-drug interaction alert raised; severity shown; override requires reason; both orders not silently coexisting without alert

Title: Duplicate order detection for same lab within short window
Preconditions: Lab order placed minutes ago for same test
Test_Steps: 1. Order CBC | 2. Re-order CBC within 5 minutes | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Duplicate order warning shown with prior order time/provider; clinician can confirm or cancel; avoids unnecessary duplicate

Title: Nurse handover note continuity across shift change
Preconditions: Admitted patient; two nursing shifts
Test_Steps: 1. Shift 1 nurse documents handover note | 2. Shift 2 nurse opens patient | 3. Verify handover visible | 4. Shift 2 adds note
Expected_Result: Handover note visible to incoming shift under same visit; chronological notes preserved; authorship attributed per nurse

Title: NPO/diet order state respected by meal scheduling
Preconditions: Admitted patient; diet order capability
Test_Steps: 1. Set diet order NPO | 2. Verify meal suppressed | 3. Change to regular diet | 4. Verify meals scheduled | 5. Set fluid-restricted
Expected_Result: Each diet state drives correct meal behavior; NPO suppresses meals; transitions audited

Title: Vitals trend chart aggregates multi-encounter observations
Preconditions: Admitted patient with vitals recorded over several encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Record vitals at 3 timepoints | 2. Open vitals trend/graph | 3. Verify all points plotted in order
Expected_Result: Trend chart plots all vitals chronologically across encounters; correct values; gaps handled gracefully

Title: Mass-casualty rapid registration throughput
Preconditions: Emergency mode; multiple simultaneous arrivals
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly register 10 patients in succession | 2. Triage each | 3. Observe ID uniqueness and queue integrity
Expected_Result: All patients receive unique IDs; no collision; queue integrity maintained; no data bleed between concurrent records

Title: ER vitals reassessment interval enforced by acuity
Preconditions: ER patients at varying acuity awaiting reassessment
Test_Steps: 1. Triage ESI-1 patient | 2. Triage ESI-4 patient | 3. Observe reassessment due timers
Test_Data: ESI-1 reassess 5 min; ESI-4 reassess 60 min
Expected_Result: Reassessment timers driven by acuity; overdue reassessments flagged; lower acuity longer interval

Title: Inbound referral creates patient context and pre-populates visit
Preconditions: Inbound referral received from external facility
Test_Steps: 1. Receive inbound referral | 2. Match/create patient | 3. Accept referral | 4. Start visit pre-populated with referral reason
Expected_Result: Inbound referral creates/matches patient; accepted referral seeds visit reason; provenance of external source retained

Title: Referral with missing target facility rejected at submission
Preconditions: Referral form open
Test_Steps: 1. Fill reason only | 2. Leave target facility blank | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Submission blocked with validation error on required target facility; referral not created

Title: Order signed by provider carries correct ordering provider on bill
Preconditions: Clinician places billable order; provider differs from data entry clerk
Test_Steps: 1. Clerk enters order on behalf of clinician | 2. Clinician signs | 3. Generate bill | 4. Verify ordering provider attribution
Expected_Result: Bill and order attribute the ordering provider correctly, not the data entry user; audit shows both

Title: Insurance eligibility checked before billing chargeable services
Preconditions: Patient with insurance plan; eligibility service configured
Test_Steps: 1. Verify active insurance | 2. Order covered service | 3. Order non-covered service | 4. Generate bill with copay rules
Expected_Result: Covered service billed to insurer with copay; non-covered billed to patient; eligibility status drives split correctly

Title: Patient merge consolidates visits, orders, and audit trails
Preconditions: Two duplicate patient records each with visits
Test_Steps: 1. Identify duplicate records | 2. Execute merge (preferred + non-preferred) | 3. Verify visits/orders/observations consolidated | 4. Verify non-preferred voided with pointer
Expected_Result: All clinical data re-pointed to preferred patient; non-preferred voided not deleted; audit retains merge provenance; no data loss

Title: Timezone consistency across encounters spanning DST/midnight
Preconditions: Visit spanning midnight; server and client timezone configured
Test_Steps: 1. Record encounter at 23:50 | 2. Record encounter at 00:10 next day | 3. Verify ordering and date display
Expected_Result: Encounters ordered correctly across midnight; stored in consistent timezone; display localized without off-by-one date errors

Title: FHIR Bundle transaction posts full encounter atomically
Preconditions: FHIR R4 transaction endpoint enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a transaction Bundle (Encounter + Observations + Condition) | 2. Introduce one invalid resource in second bundle | 3. Verify atomicity
Expected_Result: Valid bundle commits all resources; invalid bundle rolls back entirely (no partial commit); references resolved

Title: FHIR Patient $everything returns complete journey scoped to authorization
Preconditions: Patient with full journey; FHIR enabled; scoped token
Test_Steps: 1. Call Patient/{id}/$everything with authorized scope | 2. Verify resources returned | 3. Call with unauthorized scope
Expected_Result: Authorized call returns complete linked resources; unauthorized scope denied; no resources outside patient compartment leaked

Title: HL7 v2 message with malformed segment rejected and NACKed
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface active
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT message with missing required PID-3 | 2. Observe interface response | 3. Check error queue
Expected_Result: Malformed message rejected with AR/AE acknowledgement (NACK); message quarantined in error queue; not partially ingested

Title: Session timeout mid-journey preserves no unsaved PHI in client
Preconditions: Clinician mid-encounter entry; session timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Begin entering encounter data | 2. Idle until session timeout | 3. Attempt to save | 4. Re-authenticate
Expected_Result: On timeout, save blocked and re-auth required; unsaved PHI not persisted insecurely; re-auth returns to safe state

Title: Privilege escalation attempt during order signing denied
Preconditions: Nurse account lacking ordering privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Nurse opens order form via direct navigation | 2. Attempt to place/sign drug order | 3. Observe server enforcement
Expected_Result: Server denies order signing regardless of client navigation; no privilege escalation; attempt audited

Title: Keyboard-only completion of vitals-to-order workflow
Preconditions: Clinician on active visit; keyboard only (no mouse)
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to vitals form via keyboard | 2. Enter vitals | 3. Tab to diagnosis | 4. Place order entirely via keyboard | 5. Verify focus order
Expected_Result: Entire workflow completable via keyboard; logical focus order; no keyboard traps; all controls reachable and operable

Title: Screen reader announces critical lab alert in journey
Preconditions: Screen reader active; critical result present
Test_Steps: 1. Enable screen reader | 2. Navigate to result with critical flag | 3. Verify alert announced | 4. Verify ARIA live region
Expected_Result: Critical alert announced by screen reader via ARIA live region; severity conveyed non-visually; not color-only

Title: REST API drives full outpatient journey programmatically
Preconditions: REST API credentials; sufficient privileges
Test_Steps: 1. POST patient | 2. POST visit | 3. POST encounter with obs | 4. POST order | 5. POST result | 6. GET reconstructed journey
Expected_Result: Each call returns expected 2xx with resource UUIDs; references resolve; GET reconstructs full journey identical to UI-created flow

Title: API rejects order referencing ended visit
Preconditions: Ended visit exists; REST API access
Test_Steps: 1. End a visit | 2. POST order/encounter referencing that visit UUID | 3. Observe response
Expected_Result: API returns 4xx with clear error; no encounter/order attached to ended visit; integrity preserved

Title: Referential integrity of obs to concept across journey
Preconditions: DB read access in test env; journey data present
Test_Steps: 1. Query obs rows for journey | 2. Verify each obs concept_id resolves | 3. Verify encounter_id and person_id consistent | 4. Check for orphan obs
Expected_Result: All obs reference valid concept, encounter, and person; no orphan obs; voided rows retain referential pointers

Title: Follow-up appointment auto-suggested at checkout from diagnosis
Preconditions: Visit with chronic diagnosis; scheduling enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Record chronic diagnosis | 2. Proceed to checkout | 3. Verify follow-up suggestion | 4. Book follow-up
Expected_Result: Follow-up appointment suggested based on diagnosis protocol; booking links to patient; appears on schedule

Title: Result-pending checkout warns before closing visit
Preconditions: Active visit with lab order still pending result
Test_Steps: 1. Order lab | 2. Attempt checkout before result returns | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: System warns of pending results before closing visit; allows deferred-result handling; no silent loss of pending order linkage

Title: Transfer-out to external facility ends visit with disposition
Preconditions: Admitted patient requiring external transfer
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate transfer-out | 2. Record receiving facility and disposition | 3. End visit | 4. Verify bed released
Expected_Result: Visit ends with transfer disposition; receiving facility recorded; bed released; reporting reflects transfer-out

Title: Deceased patient pathway sets status and stops active orders
Preconditions: Admitted patient; death recording enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Record death with datetime and cause | 2. Verify patient marked deceased | 3. Verify active orders auto-discontinued | 4. Verify visit closed
Expected_Result: Patient marked deceased; active drug orders discontinued; visit closed; subsequent clinical orders blocked; audit complete

Title: Exploratory: rapid back-navigation during multi-step order entry
Preconditions: Clinician mid multi-step order entry
Test_Steps: 1. Begin order entry wizard | 2. Use browser back mid-step | 3. Re-enter and resubmit | 4. Observe for duplicates or partial saves
Expected_Result: No duplicate or partial orders created from back-navigation; state recovers cleanly; no orphaned draft committed

Title: Pairwise insurance x service x provider-network billing outcomes
Preconditions: Multiple insurers, service types, and network statuses configured
Test_Steps: 1. Run pairwise (insurer x service x in/out-of-network) | 2. Generate bill per combo | 3. Verify coverage split
Expected_Result: Each combination produces correct coverage split and patient responsibility; out-of-network rules applied; no mis-billing

Title: Consent status gates data sharing in referral journey
Preconditions: Patient referral with consent module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt referral data share without consent | 2. Capture consent | 3. Retry share | 4. Revoke consent and attempt again
Expected_Result: Sharing blocked without consent; allowed when granted; revocation re-blocks; all consent decisions audited

Title: Negative: prescribe contraindicated drug in pregnancy blocked
Preconditions: Female patient flagged pregnant; teratogenic drug in formulary
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm pregnancy status | 2. Order teratogenic drug | 3. Observe contraindication alert | 4. Document override reason if permitted
Expected_Result: Pregnancy contraindication alert raised before finalize; high-severity warning; override requires reason and is audited

Title: Renal-adjusted dosing prompt for impaired renal function
Preconditions: Patient with documented low eGFR; renally-cleared drug
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm eGFR value | 2. Order renally-cleared drug at standard dose | 3. Observe adjustment prompt | 4. Apply adjusted dose
Expected_Result: Renal impairment prompts dose adjustment or contraindication per eGFR band; standard dose flagged; adjusted dose accepted

Title: Smoke: core journey endpoints reachable post-deploy
Preconditions: Fresh deployment; smoke accounts available
Test_Steps: 1. Login | 2. Open registration | 3. Open find-patient | 4. Open active visit dashboard | 5. Open order entry
Expected_Result: All core journey screens load without error; key actions accessible; no broken navigation in critical path

Title: Partial outage: lab interface down queues orders for retry
Preconditions: Lab interface temporarily unavailable
Test_Steps: 1. Place lab order while interface down | 2. Observe queue behavior | 3. Restore interface | 4. Verify order delivered
Expected_Result: Order queued not lost during outage; delivered on restoration; provider informed of delay; no duplicate on retry
